mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates and reduces inflation expectations, leading to a decline in the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward yields rise in >60% of easing episodes (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth signals robust aggregate demand, raising real rate expectations and inflation pressures, which pushes the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the subsequent 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate exceeds 2.5% in the latest quarter
failure_condition: If GDP growth >2.5% but 60-day forward yields fall in more than half of the triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate signals labor market weakness, increasing recession fears and expectations of monetary easing, which drives the 10-year Treasury yield downward over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months
failure_condition: If yields rise in more than 40% of triggered instances despite unemployment spike, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in industrial production indicates weakening supply-side activity, lowering growth expectations and reducing demand for capital, which pushes the 10-year Treasury yield downward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If yields rise in more than 40% of instances with INDPRO decline >0.5% m/m, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When both inflation is elevated and the federal funds rate is below the inflation rate, the market expects the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, which raises the 10-year Treasury yield as term premiums increase to compensate for future rate hikes.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year > 3% and FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL year-over-year
failure_condition: If yields fall in more than 50% of triggered instances, or joint Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts to a high level signals robust residential investment and consumer demand, boosting growth expectations and leading to a near-term rise in the 10-year Treasury yield over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If yields fall in more than 50% of instances with HOUST > 1.5M, or 5-day forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle when the federal funds rate is already elevated and still rising, further increases in the funds rate push the 10-year Treasury yield upward as the market prices in higher short-term rates and term premiums.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS > 4% and rising over the last 3 months)
failure_condition: If during this regime yields fall in more than 40% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained high inflation, as measured by the Consumer Price Index, consistently increases the term premium demanded by investors in long-term bonds, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise over a 60-trading-day horizon regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: If inflation rises but 60-day forward yields fall in more than 40% of months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 5-year rolling window.
